title: Migrate CUDA custom extensions to real Ascend C/CANN OPP with ACLNN PyTorch bridge
symptom: Phase 5 rejects a CUDA custom-op migration when the project only swaps build plumbing to NpuExtension, CppExtension, or ATen-style adapter code. Validation reports missing native OPP producer evidence such as op_host, op_kernel, generated ACLNN headers, kernel_meta, libcust_opapi.so, build/install provenance, or same-run per-operator route evidence.
root_cause: A PyTorch NpuExtension adapter is only bridge evidence. It does not prove that the CUDA kernel was migrated to a native Ascend C/CANN custom operator. The migration is production-ready only when a project-local Ascend OPP package provides op_host metadata, AscendC kernels, generated ACLNN APIs, installed runtime artifacts, and a PyTorch bridge that links cust_opapi/opapi/ascendcl and launches ACLNN on the current NPU stream.
fix_steps: 1. Inventory every CUDA custom-op unit discovered from the original extension sources and keep a one-to-one manifest entry for each unit that must be closed by native Ascend C/CANN evidence.
2. Create a project-local Ascend OPP package, for example under `pointnet2_ops/ascend_opp`, with separate `op_host` and `op_kernel` directories for the migrated operators.

3. For each custom op, implement an `op_host` definition that declares inputs, outputs, supported dtypes and formats, attributes, shape inference, dtype inference, AICore tiling, and an explicit `AddConfig` target such as `ascend910b`.
4. For each custom op, implement an AscendC `op_kernel` source with an `extern "C" __global__ __aicore__` kernel entry matching the generated custom-op API route.

5. Build and install the OPP package so the validation artifacts include generated ACLNN headers, generated op_api library files, kernel metadata, `libcust_opapi.so`, and CMake/build/install provenance. Do not count the PyTorch adapter itself as the OPP producer.
6. Replace the runtime extension build with a PyTorch `NpuExtension` adapter that includes the generated ACLNN headers, CANN headers, and project helper headers.
7. Link the adapter against `cust_opapi`, `opapi`, and `ascendcl`, and add runtime library paths for the project OPP `op_api/lib`, CANN `lib64`, and torch_npu `lib` directories.

8. In the adapter, validate that inputs are defined NPU tensors with the expected dtype and rank, make them contiguous, allocate outputs on the same NPU device, and use `at::DeviceGuard` before launching the operator.
9. Convert PyTorch tensors to `aclTensor` objects with shape, stride, storage offset, `ACL_FORMAT_ND`, ACL dtype, and the tensor data pointer; release each `aclTensor` after use.

10. Before calling generated ACLNN APIs, register the project OPP resources once, request workspace size and executor from the generated `GetWorkspaceSize` function, allocate byte workspace when needed, launch the generated ACLNN function on `c10_npu::getCurrentNPUStream(...).stream()`, then destroy the executor and check every ACLNN status.

11. Run Phase 5 validation and require same-run, per-operator, non-empty route evidence correlated to each custom-op unit. The final gate must reject fallback, stub, report-only, direct-only, ATen-only, or adapter-only success.
12. Confirm final gate counters close all discovered units: inventory count equals manifest entries, every entry is closed/pass, remaining entries are zero, and full migration status is `FULL_PASS`.
affected_patterns: CUDAExtension project with custom CUDA/C++ extension sources
Migration attempt that only replaces CUDAExtension with NpuExtension
ATen-only or CppExtension-style adapter code used as proof of migration
Missing `op_host`, `op_kernel`, generated ACLNN headers, `kernel_meta`, `libcust_opapi.so`, or OPP build/install provenance
Runtime bridge that does not launch generated ACLNN APIs on the current NPU stream

code_changes.before:
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import CUDAExtension

ext_modules=[
    CUDAExtension(
        name="pointnet2_ops._ext",
        sources=cuda_sources,
    )
]
code_changes.after:
from torch.utils.cpp_extension import BuildExtension
import torch_npu
from torch_npu.utils.cpp_extension import NpuExtension

_opp_root = osp.join(this_dir, "pointnet2_ops", "ascend_opp")
_opp_build_out = osp.join(_opp_root, "build_out")
_cann_root = os.environ.get("ASCEND_CANN_PACKAGE_PATH", "/usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest")
_torch_npu_root = osp.dirname(osp.abspath(torch_npu.__file__))

ext_modules=[
    NpuExtension(
        name="pointnet2_ops._ext",
        sources=[
            osp.join(_ext_src_root, "src", "pointnet2_aclnn_bind.cpp"),
            osp.join(_ext_src_root, "src", "pytorch_npu_helper.cpp"),
        ],
        include_dirs=[
            osp.join(_ext_src_root, "include"),
            osp.join(_opp_build_out, "op_api", "include"),
            osp.join(_cann_root, "include"),
        ],
        library_dirs=[
            osp.join(_opp_build_out, "op_api", "lib"),
            osp.join(_cann_root, "lib64"),
            osp.join(_torch_npu_root, "lib"),
        ],
        libraries=["cust_opapi", "opapi", "ascendcl"],
        extra_compile_args=["-O2", "-std=c++17"],
    )
]

code_changes.before: // Adapter-only or ATen-only route without generated ACLNN headers, OPP resource registration, aclTensor conversion, workspace/executor handling, or current NPU stream launch.
// No conversion from at::Tensor to aclTensor for generated ACLNN APIs.
// Missing native OPP op_host definition, shape inference, dtype inference, tiling, and AICore config.

tools_needed: Ascend CANN toolkit with AscendC available
Phase 5 custom-op OPP preflight and final gate validator
antipatterns: Do NOT treat `NpuExtension` build success as proof that the CUDA kernel was migrated to native Ascend C/CANN OPP.
Do NOT accept CppExtension, ATen-only, direct-only, fallback, stub, or report-only routes as production-ready custom-op migration evidence.
Do NOT skip project-local OPP producer artifacts: `op_host`, `op_kernel`, generated ACLNN headers, `kernel_meta`, `libcust_opapi.so`, and build/install provenance are required.
Do NOT launch generated ACLNN APIs without using the current NPU stream from torch_npu.
Do NOT provide route evidence that is stale, cross-run, empty, or not correlated to each custom-op unit identity.

meta.refiner_reasoning: The artifacts show a CUDAExtension project with 9 PointNet++ custom-op units, strict constraints requiring the regular Ascend custom-op route, Phase 5 success after 12 iterations, and final gate FULL_PASS only after native Ascend C/CANN OPP producer evidence existed separately from NpuExtension adapter evidence. The source files show the reusable implementation pattern: project-local `ascend_opp`, op_host shape/dtype/tiling/AddConfig definitions, AscendC op_kernel source, a torch_npu NpuExtension bridge linked to `cust_opapi`, `opapi`, and `ascendcl`, aclTensor conversion helpers, OPP resource registration, workspace/executor management, and launch on the current NPU stream.

signals: PyTorch project contains CUDAExtension or custom CUDA/C++ extension sources
migration attempt only swaps to NpuExtension, CppExtension, or ATen-style adapter code
validation reports missing op_host, op_kernel, generated ACLNN headers, kernel_meta, libcust_opapi.so, or OPP build/install provenance
final gate rejects fallback, stub, report-only, or direct-only route evidence
required_artifacts: op_host definitions with shape inference, dtype inference, tiling, and AddConfig
generated ACLNN headers and op_api library
PyTorch NpuExtension adapter linked against cust_opapi, opapi, and ascendcl
anti_patterns: treating NpuExtension adapter evidence as the OPP producer
accepting ATen-only or fallback success as native migration
runtime bridge that does not launch ACLNN on the current NPU stream

classifier.reasoning: This candidate is an actionable reusable procedure for PyTorch CUDA custom-op migrations: adapter-only NpuExtension, CppExtension, or ATen routes are insufficient unless backed by real Ascend C/CANN OPP producer artifacts and an ACLNN bridge that loads and launches native kernels on the NPU stream. The experience should guide agents through creating op_host/op_kernel/generated OPP assets, linking cust_opapi/opapi/ascendcl, converting tensors to aclTensor, and validating same-run native execution evidence rather than merely documenting a case or applying a mechanical rewrite.

signals: PyTorch project contains CUDAExtension or custom CUDA/C++ extension sources
migration attempt only swaps to NpuExtension, CppExtension, or ATen-style adapter code
validation reports missing op_host, op_kernel, generated ACLNN headers, kernel_meta, libcust_opapi.so, or OPP build/install provenance
final gate rejects fallback, stub, report-only, or direct-only route evidence

PyTorch NpuExtension adapter linked against cust_opapi, opapi, and ascendcl

problem_description: A PyTorch CUDA custom extension is not truly migrated by only swapping to NpuExtension or ATen/CppExtension-style adapter code. Phase 5 detected missing op_host/op_kernel/generated OPP evidence and stale NpuExtension/CppExtension/ATen-only route signals, which would allow non-native or fallback success.
rough_fix_approach: Create a project-local Ascend C/CANN OPP package with op_host shape/tiling definitions, AscendC op_kernel sources, CMake/build/install provenance, generated ACLNN headers, kernel_meta, and libcust_opapi.so. Then build a PyTorch NpuExtension adapter that links cust_opapi/opapi/ascendcl, converts tensors to aclTensor, launches ACLNN on the current NPU stream, and validates runtime loading of the OPP artifact.
name: cuda-extension-to-ascendc-opp-aclnn-bridge
